commands.review_summary:
The branch scopes `startSshPortForward`'s preferred-port availability check to `127.0.0.1` and adds a Vitest regression asserting the scoped call.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +28. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main still probes the preferred SSH tunnel port without a host while the rest of the tunnel path uses `127.0.0.1`. The PR body also supplies real `node:net` output comparing the old hostless probe with the new scoped probe on a dual-stack macOS setup.

target.title: fix(ssh): scope tunnel port preflight to loopback (#94603)
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #94379 / #94394. `startSshPortForward` in `src/infra/ssh-tunnel.ts` called `ensurePortAvailable(localPort)` host-less. On a dual-stack host a bare listen binds the IPv6 wildcard `::` and misses an IPv4-only occupant, so the preflight reports a busy port as free — the exact address-family flaw fixed for the Chrome CDP caller in #94394.

Every other probe in this module is already pinned to IPv4 loopback:
- `pickEphemeralPort` → `listen(0, "127.0.0.1")`
- `canConnectLocal` → `connect({ host: "127.0.0.1" })`
- the forward itself → `-L ${localPort}:127.0.0.1:${remotePort}`

The one-line fix scopes the preflight to the interface this caller actually owns, matching #94394's caller-scoped pattern:

```ts
await ensurePortAvailable(localPort, "127.0.0.1");
```

Lower harm than the Chrome path (only affects SSH-tunnel preferred-port selection, and it already falls back to an ephemeral port on `EADDRINUSE`), but same root cause and internally consistent.

Closes #94603

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `startSshPortForward`'s host-less port preflight binds the IPv6 wildcard on dual-stack hosts and fails to detect an IPv4-only occupant of the preferred port, reporting a busy port as free (same root cause as #94379).

**Real environment tested:** macOS (darwin arm64, dual-stack), Node v26, run directly against `node:net` to reproduce the OS-level bind behavior the function relies on.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** bound an occupant IPv4-only on `127.0.0.1:<port>`, then compared the old host-less preflight against the new `127.0.0.1`-scoped preflight via real `net.createServer().listen()` calls:

**Evidence after fix:**
```
occupant bound IPv4-only on 127.0.0.1:51743
OLD host-less preflight (binds :: wildcard): SUCCEEDED -> reports FREE  [FALSE NEGATIVE]
NEW scoped preflight (127.0.0.1):            EADDRINUSE -> BUSY -> EADDRINUSE branch [CORRECT]
```

**Observed result after fix:** the scoped preflight gets `EADDRINUSE` against the IPv4-only occupant and correctly enters the ephemeral-port fallback branch, where the old host-less probe silently reported the port free. Added regression test in `src/infra/ssh-tunnel.test.ts` asserts the preflight is called with `(port, "127.0.0.1")`.

**What was not tested:** a full live SSH connection (the spawn path is unchanged by this patch); the fix is confined to the preflight host argument.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/infra/ssh-tunnel.test.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts` — 18/18 pass
- [x] `pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/ssh-tunnel.ts src/infra/ssh-tunnel.test.ts` — clean
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — exit 0
- [x] live `node:net` repro above

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
